key_insight: Precursor grain structure controls graphite-to-diamond conversion by enabling local diamond nucleation at grain boundaries while blocking further growth at mismatched boundaries, creating kinetically arrested mixed sp2-sp3 states.
engineering_relevance: This means graphite crystallinity and grain size can be used as a practical process variable to steer HPHT synthesis toward diamond, mixed nanocomposites, or arrested intermediate products.
method: Large-scale molecular dynamics simulations of graphite-to-diamond transformation under HPHT conditions
Comparison of precursor microstructures, including single-crystal-like domains versus heterogeneous grain structures
Mechanistic analysis of nucleation and propagation behavior at grain boundaries
evidence_signal: The simulations show that heterogeneous graphite stabilizes mixed diamond-graphite nanocomposites, while large or single-crystalline domains undergo more cooperative bulk transformation to diamond.
watch_item: The conclusions come from atomistic simulation, so real synthesis may still depend on pressure, temperature, kinetics, and sample-specific defects beyond grain structure alone.
paper_title: Grain-boundary-mediated kinetic arrest in graphite-to-diamond transformation
